mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, driving positive equity valuations through increased corporate earnings expectations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2015 payroll level at 142,288k indicates sustained hiring momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero (below 0.25%) signals continued monetary accommodation that suppresses discount rates and sustains equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as liquidity-driven momentum persists.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2015 FEDFUNDS at 0.14% confirms ultra-low rate environment
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.1% or t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative equity returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% and RSAFS declines > 3% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 HOUST at 1,206k and RSAFS at 447,715M show demand resilience
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS decline as specified, 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 (fails to show negative correlation).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1% month-over-month, signaling expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2015 INDPRO at 107.08 reflects post-crisis manufacturing recovery
failure_condition: When INDPRO grows > 1% month-over-month, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean < 0.05% or t-test p > 0.20), or Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) declines or remains flat month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) stays above 5%, signaling a disinflationary environment that permits continued monetary accommodation and supports equity valuations through lower real discount rates over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 CPI at 238.10 and August 2015 UNRATE at 5.1% indicate disinflationary slack
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 60-day forward returns do not exceed baseline returns by > 50 bps on average, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.2% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are cut.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2015 UNRATE at 5.1% near post-crisis lows
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month, 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 (fails to show negative correlation).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter decelerates below 2% annualized rate signals weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate revenue growth, producing negative equity returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2015 GDPC1 at 16,324.3B reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
failure_condition: When GDPC1 annualized growth < 2%, 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean > 0% or t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC > -0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously signals stagflationary pressures and supply-side weakness, producing negative equity returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress and growth expectations deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly change < -0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:August 2015 INDPRO at 107.08 and UNRATE at 5.1% show no stagflationary signal currently
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 (fails to show negative correlation).
